title: pittosporum rangitahua
intro: Pittosporum rangitahua, also known as Kawakawa or Poro, is a small tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum rangitahua are attributed to its leaves, which have been traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Pittosporum rangitahua is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and topical applications that can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
